title: Automotive | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
Fragomen has represented major players in every sector of the automotive industry for more than 40 years, including automobile and equipment manufacturers, supply chain partners, and related technology and finance companies.
We’ve partnered with clients through a myriad of innovations and disruptions that have characterized the sector over the past several decades.
As a result, we are intimately acquainted with the immigration challenges faced by the industry today. We use this experience to anticipate shifts in our clients’ needs and execute solutions for them quickly and efficiently—a must in this competitive business environment.
Industry leaders and new entrants alike rely on us for our:
Electric cars, autonomous vehicles and other novel technologies are revolutionizing the industry. These novel technologies have created sophisticated employee roles that do not fit neatly into traditional job categories. We’re working with clients to explore visa options for these new employees and educate regulators about their unique needs.
Fragomen’s automotive industry clients are inventing, manufacturing and marketing products across borders, in cooperation with multiple corporate entities and government authorities. Because we represent clients in every region of the globe, we help clients adjust their immigration posture to serve whatever jurisdictional configuration their business strategy demands. And our singular focus on immigration means that our service is reliable, consistent, and predictable worldwide.

### Understanding of local issues
Even as globalization continues in its pace, governments are instituting restrictions designed to protect local economies and workforces. At the same time, local companies are sprouting up in every region, often in response to the technical requirements of novel technologies (electrification and battery programs, for example). We understand the idiosyncrasies of local governments and markets and how they can impact our clients.

### Government strategies savvy
Whether our clients are negotiating a cross-border deal involving national government oversight or working with city or state officials to execute a local project, they are backed by Fragomen’s celebrated Government Strategies and Compliance group.
An unexpected change in local immigration policy can significantly deflate the value of any deal, rendering months of planning useless. Our clients often consult with us before they strike a deal or launch a project. As business counselors, we help them make sound choices that can protect value over the long term—and uncover unexpected strategic opportunities.
Sound immigration strategy is a mission-critical component of automotive industry transactions; cross-border transactions often involve significant transfer and reallocation of personnel. We boast decades of experience helping clients protect their human assets in every kind of deal, including divestitures, mergers, spin-offs, restructurings, joint ventures, partnerships and limited-duration projects. We help our clients take advantage of special visa provisions where available, for example, to make short-term engagements for vehicle launches or car testing programs cost-effective and easy to deploy.

### Actionable industry analysis
As the world’s largest immigration firm, with an incredibly diverse client base, we have vast immigration data at our fingertips. This allows us to conduct sophisticated Trends Analysis and Benchmarking specifically focused on the automotive sector.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
